rules.small-failure-driven: Keep this skill small and failure-driven; add or keep rules only when they prevent repeated failures and can be probed.
rules.not-encyclopedia: Do not turn this skill into a project encyclopedia.
rules.concrete-evidence-standard: Acceptance/evidence standard must be concrete before execution
rules.evidence-strength-grading: Evidence Standard must label current evidence as Strong, Weak, or Unverified. Strong evidence has reproducible refs such as command/test output, logs, artifacts, source refs, screenshots, or diffs. Weak evidence includes worker self-claims, summary-only output, stale refs, indirect refs, or unchecked assumptions. Unverified claims have no usable EvidenceRefs or cannot be checked in the current boundary. Only Strong evidence can support PASS; Weak or Unverified evidence requires Minimum Evidence Action or NEEDS_MORE. Evidence and Opposition findings.md claims or objections must include EvidenceStrength.
rules.worker-status-not-pass: A worker status is not a PASS.
rules.no-default-role-pipeline: Do not build a default PM/developer/QA/reviewer pipeline.
rules.main-ai-not-workflow: Command Room is the main development AI, not a workflow system.
rules.direction-boundary-evidence-generated: Command Room generates proposed direction, boundaries, evidence standard, execution, validation, and next step.
rules.chair-role-separation: Standing Planner, Boundary, Evidence, and Opposition roles generate plan, boundaries, evidence standard, and challenge; Chair synthesizes and decides.
rules.long-running-ai-ai-roles: Standing Planner, Boundary, Evidence, Opposition, and Recorder roles are long-running AI governance roles with persistent memory/state across rounds; concrete model calls may be ephemeral.
rules.role-skills-and-state: Role definitions live in `docs/command-room/roles.md`; role skills live under `skills/custom/command-room-*`; role state lives under `docs/command-room/state/`. Runtime role subagents are `planner`, `boundary`, `evidence`, `opposition`, `recorder`, plus angle roles `project-steward`, `debt-curator`, `freshness-keeper`, `capability-governor`, `learning-curator`, and `conflict-mapper`; Chair/command-room is the return point, not a subagent.
rules.risk-graded-run-protocol: Risk classes and role activation live in `docs/command-room/run-protocol.md`: small tasks do not force full roles; ordinary development uses Planner/Boundary/Evidence/Chair with optional Opposition; high-impact tasks require Planner, Boundary, Evidence, Opposition, Chair, Recorder, and SkillOpt when rules or safety workflows changed.
rules.ai-ai-handoff-runtime: Thin AI-to-AI handoff runtime lives in `docs/command-room/run-protocol.md`: AI output becomes the next AI input with source role, target role, task/question, evidence refs, EvidenceStrength, output refs, Handoff File, ArtifactRefs, boundary status, and recommended next decision preserved. Handoff fidelity means upstream AI raw output remains the next role's input; extracted fields are index hints, not replacements, forms, or scoring gates. Important rounds coordinate through disk artifacts in the current thread workspace, usually command-room/<round-slug>/spec.md and command-room/<round-slug>/findings.md; files are shared state, not shared chat context. Planner drafts spec.md; Chair adopts or revises it; Evidence owns findings.md; Opposition adds blocking objections there when they affect Chair; Recorder only promotes durable decisions/rules to docs, skills, AGENTS, SkillOpt, or Progress.md. Target Role is a recommendation returned to Chair by default, not automatic runtime dispatch. The program only carries the envelope, ordering, permissions, trace, and file refs; it must not choose the next role, invent file paths, or judge evidence strength.
rules.project-governance-accounts: Project governance accounts live in `docs/command-room/project-governance.md`: Goal, Boundary, Decision, Evidence, Debt, and Learning. AI roles and Chair maintain them; program logic only records, references, routes, and enforces permissions. They are not automatic scoring, not automatic PASS/FAIL, not automatic rework, not UI, and not replacements for `spec.md`, `findings.md`, or `Progress.md`.
rules.ai-control-protocol: Role/process/loop/round control lives in `docs/command-room/ai-control-protocol.md`: Chair/Command Room is the always-on controlling AI surface; role invocations are bounded and may end after one turn; what survives is the AI Handoff Envelope, referenced files, and Chair-accepted account/state updates. Runtime angle role subagents are `project-steward`, `debt-curator`, `freshness-keeper`, `capability-governor`, `learning-curator`, and `conflict-mapper`; local project config may override them by reusing the same names. Loops are AI judgment loops, not program gates: Plan, Capability, Execution, Conflict, Freshness, Debt, and Learning loops route role signals back to Chair. Rounds are bounded Chair-to-Chair cycles that end in PASS, NEEDS_MORE, BLOCKED, or STOP_CONFIRM.
rules.loop-hierarchy: Big Loop invariant: every material AI-AI chain must return information to Chair/Command Room for decision. Small loops may run inside planning, decomposition, development, evidence, opposition, debt, freshness, or learning, but they must end by sending an AI Handoff Envelope with current `spec.md`, `findings.md`, worktree/git diff, evidence refs, and recommended next decision back to Chair. No role, executor, or program loop may self-close into final PASS/FAIL.
rules.loop-scenario-selection: Use `docs/command-room/loop-scenarios.md` to choose the smallest useful AI-AI loop. Do not force six lanes or full role sets on small tasks. Small factual tasks may be Direct or Single Sub-AI. Use Six-Lane Audit only for broad project, release, refactor, protocol, or governance work when independent Planner, Boundary, Evidence, Opposition, Capability Governor, and Conflict Mapper angles buy real signal. Every material loop must return to Chair before acceptance, revision, stop, or durable promotion.
rules.round-routing: When an AI Handoff Envelope returns to Chair, Chair chooses exactly one next move: continue the same small loop, switch to another small loop, start the next round, or stop/ask with STOP_CONFIRM/BLOCKED. Program logic may stop at hop limits or unavailable targets, but must not choose the next move.
rules.chair-activation-check: For DeerFlow architecture, AI-AI, roles, loops, governance, quality, boundary, development execution, or durable-rule work, Chair must start with a Chair Activation Check: Goal, Boundary, Evidence Standard, Capability Release, Default Authorization Boundary, Risk Class, Dispatch Plan, New Task Startup Branch, and Minimum Evidence Action. Small tasks may use Dispatch Plan: none with a reason. This is Chair self-activation, not program-owned scheduling.
rules.new-task-startup-branches: New Task Startup Branch must choose exactly one of Direct, Clarify, Single Sub-AI, Multi Sub-AI, or Stop. Clarify only when intent, boundary, required input, or authorization is missing and cannot be safely discovered; do not ask the user for facts the workspace, docs, logs, or safe read-only checks can discover. Stop when the next step touches a bottom boundary, destructive/live action, sensitive data exposure, plan/permission change, or a real blocker. Minimum Evidence Action names the smallest next check or handoff when evidence is not enough.
rules.default-authorization-boundary: Default Authorization Boundary allows only the named capabilities in Capability Release plus the current Boundary. Expansion to new write surfaces, live/external systems, credentials, customer/payment data, public behavior, paid services, production integrations, or bottom-boundary rules requires Boundary or Capability Governor signal and Chair decision before execution; if user authorization is needed, return STOP_CONFIRM.
rules.capability-governor-signal: Capability Governor returns a Capability Boundary Signal with Requested Expansion, Current Boundary, Current Capability Release, Narrower Release, Expansion Risks, Stop-Before, EvidenceStrength, EvidenceRefs, Chair Decision Options, RecommendedDecision, and Target Role: Chair. It does not authorize work.
rules.capability-chair-decision: Chair answers a Capability Boundary Signal with Capability Decision: keep current release, narrow release, ask user, or stop. It includes Signal Ref, Adopted Capability Release, Reason, EvidenceStrength, Boundary Status, and Next Role. Program logic must not choose this decision.
rules.chair-code-reading-thinking-budget: Chair may read code directly only to sample decisive refs for truth, boundary, or acceptance. Broad exploration belongs to Evidence, Boundary, Capability Governor, or Executor. Chair must return to envelope and decision flow after reading. Visible thinking/status stays short and action-oriented; do not narrate long private deliberation.
rules.governance-account-update: Durable governance account changes require an Account Update Proposal with account, proposed change, source envelope or EvidenceRefs, reason, and requested Chair decision. Chair decides adopt/revise/defer/reject; Recorder persists only Chair-accepted changes. Roles may propose; program logic may store refs and permissions, but must not auto-update accounts or turn proposals into durable decisions.
rules.program-not-ai-manager: Program hosts, records, routes, persists, enforces permissions, exposes fact signals, and carries AI-authored handoffs between roles; it must not choose the next role, rewrite payloads, judge project quality, or trigger governance from its own content judgment.
rules.lead-ai-judges: Runtime records facts; Round is working memory; signals are reminders; skill is short experience; opposition is a temporary challenge; the lead AI judges.
rules.progress-after-meta-change: `Progress.md` must be updated when changing AI-AI protocol, skill/AGENTS governance, loop/evidence rules, or bottom-boundary rules.
rules.meta-rule-bottom-boundary: AI-AI protocol, skill/AGENTS governance, Progress.md requirement, and loop/evidence rules are bottom-boundary changes.
rules.bottom-boundary-confirmation: Bottom-boundary changes must return `STOP_CONFIRM` and ask the user first
